title: The Odyssey
description: The Odyssey — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Odyssey" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Achilles
memes.description: Greatest of the Greek warriors at Troy, son of Peleus and the sea-nymph Thetis. A central figure in the *Iliad*, his wrath and subsequent death at Troy are frequently referenced throughout the *Odyssey* as a touchstone of heroic glory and tragic fate.

memes.label: Aeaea
memes.description: The wooded island of the enchantress Circe — a place of transformation, danger, and ultimately guidance, where Odysseus's men become swine and where the hero receives the crucial directions for his journey to the Underworld.

memes.label: Aegisthus
memes.description: The usurper who seduced Clytemnestra and murdered Agamemnon upon his return from Troy — his crime is the Odyssey's foundational warning about human folly and the consequences of ignoring divine counsel.

memes.label: Aeolia
memes.description: A floating island ruled by Aeolus, keeper of the winds, where Odysseus receives the bag of winds that nearly brings him home.

memes.label: Aeolus
memes.description: Divine keeper of the winds, ruler of the floating island Aeolia. He gives Odysseus the bag of adverse winds, but the crew's greed dooms the voyage home.

memes.label: Agamemnon
memes.description: King of Mycenae, commander of the Greek forces at Troy — murdered upon his return home by Aegisthus and Clytemnestra, his fate serves as a cautionary counterpoint to Odysseus's nostos throughout the Odyssey.

memes.label: Agelaus
memes.description: One of the leading suitors of Penelope, son of Damastor, who urges restraint after the failed bow-stringing attempt and leads the suitors' counterattack in the final battle.

memes.label: Ajax
memes.description: The two Greek heroes named Ajax — Ajax the Great (son of Telamon) and Ajax the Lesser (son of Oileus) — whose fates after the Trojan War embody the tragic returns of the Achaean heroes.

memes.label: Alcinous
memes.description: The wise and hospitable king of the Phaeacians, husband of Arete, and father of Nausicaa — whose generous reception of Odysseus and provision of safe passage to Ithaca exemplify the ideal of xenia.

memes.label: Amphinomus
memes.description: The most virtuous of Penelope's suitors, son of Nisus of Dulichium, who alone among the suitors shows kindness to Odysseus in disguise and receives a personal warning — which he tragically fails to heed.

memes.label: Anticlea
memes.description: Mother of Odysseus, whose shade Odysseus encounters in the Underworld and who reveals the circumstances of her death from grief for her absent son.

memes.label: Antilochus
memes.description: The beloved son of Nestor, killed at Troy by Memnon, whose death is mourned in the *Odyssey* with an epitaph of faultless virtue and whose remains share the burial urn of Achilles and Patroclus.

memes.label: Antinous
memes.description: The arrogant leader of Penelope's suitors, whose hubris and cruelty make him the principal human villain of the Odyssey — the first to die when Odysseus's bow finds its mark.

memes.label: Antiphus
memes.description: A loyal elder of Ithaca, companion of Halitherses, who belongs to the small remnant of Odysseus's generation that remained faithful during the suitors' occupation.

memes.label: Apollo
memes.description: The Olympian god of archery, music, and prophecy, who appears in the Odyssey as a bringer of gentle death and a divine patron of the feast day at Ithaca.

memes.label: Arete
memes.description: Queen of the Phaeacians, wife of Alcinous, and a figure of exceptional authority — uniquely honoured among women, to whom Odysseus is advised to appeal before any other in the Phaeacian court.

memes.label: The Argo
memes.description: The legendary ship of Jason and the Argonauts, famed in the Odyssey as the only vessel to have successfully navigated the Planctae (Clashing Rocks) with Juno's divine aid — the sole precedent for Odysseus's passage through perilous waters.

memes.label: Artemis
memes.description: Olympian goddess of the hunt, the wilderness, and the moon; twin sister of Apollo. Referenced in Homer's Odyssey as a model of beauty and a divine archer.

memes.label: Ate (Blind Ruin)
memes.description: The ancient Greek concept of blind ruin — the delusion or madness sent by the gods that causes mortals to rush toward their own destruction, the final stage before nemesis in the Odyssey's moral sequence.

memes.label: Athena
memes.description: Goddess of wisdom, war, and crafts — the divine patron of Odysseus whose protection and guidance shapes the entire Odyssey, delighting in the cunning intelligence she shares with her mortal favorite.

memes.label: Autolycus
memes.description: The maternal grandfather of Odysseus, a master of oaths and cunning favored by Hermes, whose naming of the infant Odysseus and role in the boar-hunt scar narrative establish the hero's inherited cleverness.

memes.label: "Book 1: Minerva's Descent to Ithaca"
memes.description: "MINERVA’S DESCENT TO ITHACA. The poem opens within forty eight days of the arrival of Ulysses in his dominions. He had now remained seven years in the Island of Calypso,"

memes.label: "Book 2: The Council of Ithaca"
memes.description: "Telemachus in the assembly of the lords of Ithaca complains of the injustice done him by the suitors, and insists upon their departure from his palace; appealing to the princes, and exciting the peopl"

memes.label: "Book 3: The Interview of Telemachus and Nestor"
memes.description: "Telemachus, guided by Pallas in the shape of Mentor, arrives in the morning at Pylos, where Nestor and his sons are sacrificing on the sea- shore to Neptune. Telemachus declares the occasion of his co"

memes.label: "Book 4: The Conference with Menelaus"
memes.description: "Telemachus with Pisistratus arriving at Sparta, is hospitably received by Menelaus to whom he relates the cause of his coming, and learns from him many particulars of what befell the Greeks since the"

memes.label: "Book 5: The Departure of Ulysses from Calypso"
memes.description: "THE DEPARTURE OF ULYSSES FROM CALYPSO Pallas in a council of the gods complains of the detention of Ulysses in the Island of Calypso: whereupon Mercury is sent to command his"

memes.description: "Pallas appearing in a dream in to Nausicaa (the daughter of Alcinous, king of Phæacia, commands her to descend to the river, and wash the robes of state, in preparation for her nuptials. Nausicaa goes"

memes.label: "Book 7: The Court of Alcinous"
memes.description: "The princess Nausicaa returns to the city and Ulysses soon after follows thither. He is met by Pallas in the form of a young virgin, who guides him to the palace, and directs him in what manner to add"

memes.label: "Book 8: The Sports and Music of the Phaeacians"
memes.description: "Alcinous calls a council, in which it is resolved to transport Ulysses into his country. After which splendid entertainments are made, where the celebrated musician and poet, Demodocus, plays and sing"

memes.label: "Book 9: The Adventures of the Cicones, Lotophagi and Cyclops"

memes.description: "ADVENTURES WITH AEOLUS, THE LAESTRYGONS, AND CIRCE. Ulysses arrives at the island of Æolus, who gives him prosperous winds, and incloses the adverse ones in a bag, which his companions"

memes.label: "Book 11: The Descent into Hell"
memes.description: "Ulysses continues his narration. How he arrived at the land of the Cimmerians, and what ceremonies he performed to invoke the dead. The manner of his descent, and the apparition of the shades: his"

memes.label: "Book 12: The Sirens, Scylla, and Charybdis"
memes.description: "THE SIRENE, SCYLLA, AND CHARYBDIS. He relates how, after his return from the shades, he was sent by Circe on his voyage, by the coast of the Sirens, and by the strait of Scylla"

memes.label: "Book 13: The Arrival of Ulysses in Ithaca"
memes.description: "Ulysses takes his leave of Alcinous and Arete, and embarks in the evening. Next morning the ship arrives at Ithaca; where the sailors, as Ulysses is yet sleeping, lay him on the shore with all his tre"

memes.label: "Book 14: The Conversation with Eumaeus"
memes.description: "Ulysses arrives in disguise at the house of Eumaeus, where he is received, entertained, and lodged with the utmost hospitality. The several discourses of that faithful old servant, with the feigned st"

memes.label: "Book 15: The Return of Telemachus"
memes.description: "The goddess Minerva commands Telemachus in a vision to return to Ithaca. Pisistratus and he take leave of Menelaus, and arrive at Pylos, where they part: and Telemachus sets sail, after having receive"

memes.label: "Book 16: The Discovery of Ulysses to Telemachus"
memes.description: "Telemachus arriving at the lodge of Eumaeus, sends him to carry Penelope the news of his return. Minerva appearing to Ulysses, commands him to discover himself to his son. The princes, who had lain in"

memes.label: "Book 17: The Return of Telemachus to the Palace"
memes.description: "Telemachus returning to the city, relates to Penelope the sum of his travels. Ulysses is conducted by Eumaeus to the palace, where his old dog Argus acknowledges his master, after an absence of twenty"

memes.label: "Book 18: The Fight of Ulysses and Irus"
memes.description: "The beggar Irus insults Ulysses; the suitors promote the quarrel, in which Irus is worsted, and miserably handled. Penelope descends, and receives the presents of the suitors. The dialogue of Ulysses"

memes.label: "Book 19: The Discovery of Ulysses to Euryclea"
memes.description: "Ulysses and his son remove the weapons out of the armoury. Ulysses, in conversation with Penelope, gives a fictitious account of his adventures; then assures her he had formerly entertained her husban"

memes.label: "Book 20: The Events Preparatory to the Suitors' Destruction"

memes.label: "Book 21: The Bending of Ulysses' Bow"
memes.description: "THE BENDING OF ULYSSES’ BOW. Penelope, to put an end to the solicitation of the suitors, proposes to marry the person who shall first bend the bow of Ulysses, and shoot"

memes.label: "Book 22: The Death of the Suitors"
memes.description: "Ulysses begins the slaughter of the suitors by the death of Antinous. He declares himself, and lets fly his arrows at the rest. Telemachus assists and brings arms for his father, himself, Eumaeus, and"

memes.label: "Book 23: The Recognition of Ulysses by Penelope"
memes.description: "Euryclea awakens Penelope with the news of Ulysses’ return, and the death of the suitors. Penelope scarcely credits her; but supposes some god has punished them, and descends from her department in do"

memes.label: "Book 24: The Conclusion"
memes.description: "The souls of the suitors are conducted by Mercury to the infernal shades. Ulysses in the country goes to the retirement of his father, Laertes; he finds him busied in his garden all alone; the manner"

memes.label: Boreas
memes.description: Greek god of the north wind, referenced in Homer's Odyssey as a powerful natural force at sea.

memes.label: Calypso
memes.description: The nymph-goddess who detained Odysseus on her island Ogygia for seven years, offering him immortality in exchange for his love — a temptation he ultimately refused in favor of mortal homecoming.

memes.label: Calypso's Island
memes.description: Odysseus's seven-year detention on the island of the goddess Calypso (Book 5), the longest single sojourn of his nostos — ending only when Hermes, sent by Zeus, commands his release, and the hero builds a raft to continue his journey home.

memes.label: Characters Index
memes.description: A complete index of all characters in Homer's Odyssey, dynamically generated from the wiki's person entries.

memes.label: Charybdis
memes.description: A monstrous sea creature dwelling beneath a rock in the strait opposite Scylla. Three times daily she swallows the sea and disgorges it, creating a deadly whirlpool. Odysseus narrowly survives her after losing his ship and crew to Zeus' thunderbolt.

memes.label: Circe
memes.description: The enchantress-goddess who transformed Odysseus's crew into swine on her island Aeaea, then became his lover and ally, providing crucial guidance for his journey to the Underworld and beyond.

memes.label: Circe's Island
memes.description: Odysseus's year-long sojourn on the island of the enchantress Circe (Book 10), where half his crew is transformed into swine, the hero overcomes her magic with Hermes's aid, and the voyage to the Underworld is first commanded.

memes.label: Clytemnestra
memes.description: Queen of Mycenae, wife of Agamemnon, who conspired with her lover Aegisthus to murder her husband upon his return from Troy — the Odyssey's archetype of the faithless wife, contrasted repeatedly with the loyal Penelope.

memes.label: Concepts Index
memes.description: A complete index of all concepts, themes, and cultural values in Homer's Odyssey, dynamically generated from the wiki's concept entries.

memes.label: Crete
memes.description: A large, populous island in the Aegean Sea with ninety cities and a mixed population of many peoples and languages; used by Odysseus as the fictional homeland in the "Cretan tales" — the false autobiographies he tells while in disguise upon returning to Ithaca.

memes.label: Ctesippus
memes.description: A crude and arrogant suitor from Same who hurls an ox-hoof at the disguised Odysseus, an act of violence against a suppliant that seals his fate in the coming slaughter.

memes.label: Demodocus
memes.description: The blind bard of the Phaeacian court, whose songs of the Trojan War and the gods move Odysseus to tears.

memes.label: Dike (Justice)
memes.description: The ancient Greek concept of justice as cosmic and social order — the proper arrangement of things that the Odyssey restores through Odysseus's return and the suitors' punishment.

memes.label: Dolius
memes.description: The aged and loyal servant of Laertes, father of the treacherous Melanthius and Melantho, who remains faithful to Odysseus's house and joins the final battle against the suitors' avenging kinsmen.

memes.label: Dolos (Deception)
memes.description: The ancient Greek concept of cunning deception — the strategic use of disguise, lies, and trickery that enables Odysseus to survive monsters, test allies, and reclaim his kingdom.

memes.label: Dulichium
memes.description: An island kingdom in the Ionian Sea, part of Odysseus's realm, contributing the largest contingent of suitors to Penelope's court.

memes.label: Elpenor
memes.description: The youngest member of Odysseus's crew, who dies in a drunken fall from Circe's roof and is the first shade Odysseus encounters in the underworld.

memes.label: Elysium
memes.description: The blessed afterlife realm at the far western edge of the world, beyond Oceanus, where favored heroes dwell in eternal peace — the destination promised to Menelaus.

memes.label: Entrance to the Underworld
memes.description: The threshold of the Underworld at the far western edge of Oceanus where Odysseus performed the blood-sacrifice ritual to summon the shades of the dead in Book 11 — the boundary between the worlds of the living and the dead.

memes.label: Eumaeus
memes.description: The faithful swineherd of Odysseus whose exemplary hospitality to the disguised king — offering all he has despite his poverty — makes him the poem's model of pistis (faithfulness) and humble xenia.

memes.label: Euryalus
memes.description: A young Phaeacian noble and champion wrestler who insults Odysseus at the games, then repents and offers a sword as reconciliation — a narrative exemplar of the xenia code's restoration.

memes.label: Euryclea
memes.description: The aged and faithful nurse of Odysseus who recognizes him by his scar during the foot-washing scene in Book 19 and aids in identifying the disloyal maidservants before the slaughter of the suitors.

memes.label: Eurycleia
memes.description: The aged nurse of Odysseus and later Telemachus, whose recognition of her master's scar in Book 19 is one of the epic's most dramatic moments — and whose loyalty to the household is absolute.

memes.label: Eurylochus
memes.description: A companion of Odysseus, second-in-command on the voyage home from Troy — known for his caution, his defiance on the island of the Sun, and his role in the Circe episode.

memes.label: Eurymachus
memes.description: The most articulate and politically astute of Penelope's suitors, second only to Antinous in prominence. A master of persuasive speech and manipulation, he presents a more dangerous face of the suitors' corruption — charming on the surface, ruthless beneath.

memes.label: Eurynome
memes.description: The faithful housekeeper of Odysseus's palace in Ithaca, who attends Penelope and Odysseus with quiet devotion after the suitors' slaughter.

memes.label: Events by Book
memes.description: A chronological catalogue of all event-type entries in the Odyssey wiki, ordered by the book of the epic in which they occur.

memes.label: Events Index
memes.description: A complete index of all events in Homer's Odyssey, dynamically generated from the wiki's event entries.

memes.label: Featured Content
memes.description: A curated showcase of premium and featured-quality pages across all types in the Odyssey wiki.

memes.label: "Front Matter"
memes.description: "Introductory material, preface, and life of Homer from Pope's translation of The Odyssey."

memes.label: Hades
memes.description: The realm of the dead at the edge of the world, visited by Odysseus in Book 11 — the only place in the Odyssey where the living and the dead meet face to face, and the structural and theological center of the epic.

memes.label: Halitherses
memes.description: The aged augur of Ithaca, who interprets the omen of the eagles at the assembly and warns the suitors of their impending doom.

memes.label: Helen
memes.description: Queen of Sparta, wife of Menelaus, and the woman whose beauty launched the Trojan War — in the Odyssey she appears as a restored queen, a perceptive hostess, and a figure of complex self-awareness about her own legendary role.

memes.label: Helios
memes.description: The Titan god of the sun, whose sacred cattle on the island of Trinacria become the occasion of the final and most catastrophic transgression of Odysseus's crew.

memes.label: Hera
memes.description: Queen of the Olympian gods and wife of Zeus, invoked twice in the Odyssey as "imperial Juno" — once as the guardian of seafarers and once as the bestower of majesty upon mortal women.

memes.label: Heracles
memes.description: The greatest hero of Greek mythology, seen by Odysseus among the shades in the Underworld. In the Odyssey, his phantom converses with Odysseus while the real Heracles feasts among the gods.

memes.label: Hermes
memes.description: The Olympian messenger god, conductor of souls to the Underworld, and divine intermediary — deliverer of Zeus's decree that frees Odysseus from Calypso.

memes.label: Horkos (Oath)
memes.description: The ancient Greek concept of the binding oath — a solemn promise witnessed by the gods whose violation brought divine punishment, central to the moral framework of the Odyssey.

memes.label: Hubris (Pride)
memes.description: The ancient Greek concept of excessive pride and violent transgression — the arrogant overstepping of mortal bounds that invites divine punishment, embodied in the Odyssey by the suitors' reckless abuse of Odysseus's household.

memes.label: Icarius
memes.description: Father of Penelope and a respected elder of Sparta, whose paternal authority and dowering of his daughter frame the political stakes of the suitors' occupation in Ithaca.

memes.label: Idomeneus
memes.description: The Cretan king who fought at Troy and returned home safely — used by Odysseus as the basis of one of his false-identity tales.

memes.label: Iliad
memes.description: Homer's epic of the Trojan War — the poem of wrath, honor, and mortal glory that forms the narrative prequel to the Odyssey and the shared reference point for every character in its story.

memes.label: Immortals and Deities
memes.description: A catalogue of divine and immortal beings in the Odyssey wiki — Olympian gods, goddesses, nymphs, and mythological entities of the Greek pantheon.

memes.label: Irus
memes.description: The arrogant beggar of Ithaca who challenges Odysseus in his beggar disguise to a boxing match, is brutally defeated, and serves as a foil displaying the disguised hero's restrained might.

memes.label: Ismarus
memes.description: A city on the Thracian coast, home of the Cicones, sacked by Odysseus and his crew immediately after leaving Troy in the first adventure of his wanderings.

memes.label: Ithaca
memes.description: The rugged island kingdom of Odysseus in the Ionian Sea — the destination of the hero's nostos, whose recovery drives the entire Odyssey and whose specific geography proves his identity at every recognition scene.

memes.label: The Ithacan Assembly
memes.description: Telemachus's convening of the first Ithacan assembly in twenty years (Book 2), where he publicly challenges the suitors before the gathered people, asserts his claim as Odysseus's heir, and demands a ship to search for news of his father — the first act of adulthood that sets the Telemachy in motion.

memes.label: Ithaca's Harbor
memes.description: The principal harbor of Ithaca, sacred to the sea-god Phorcys, sheltered by twin rocky headlands and olive groves — the landing place where the Phaeacians deposit the sleeping Odysseus.

memes.label: Jason
memes.description: Leader of the Argonauts, whose ship the Argo — the only vessel to have passed the Planctae before Odysseus — is cited by Circe as a precedent in Book XII.

memes.label: Laertes
memes.description: The aged father of Odysseus, living in grief and poverty on the margins of his own kingdom — his reunion with his son in Book 24 completes the epic's restoration of the household across three generations.

memes.label: Laodamas
memes.description: The favourite son of King Alcinous of Phaeacia, a champion athlete who invites the disguised Odysseus to join the Phaeacian games — the courteous host whose conduct models the xenia ideal that Euryalus's insult momentarily violates.

memes.label: Leucothea
memes.description: A sea goddess who aids Odysseus by giving him her magical veil (scarf) to survive Poseidon's storm.

memes.label: Maron
memes.description: Priest of Apollo at Ismarus, spared by Odysseus during the sack of the Cicones' city. He gifts Odysseus the divine wine that later proves decisive against Polyphemus.

memes.label: Medon
memes.description: The herald of Odysseus's household, spared in the slaughter of the suitors because Telemachus vouches for his innocence and his care for the young prince.

memes.label: Melanthius
memes.description: The disloyal goatherd of Odysseus's household who sides with the suitors, repeatedly insults Odysseus in disguise, and is brutally executed after the suitors' slaughter.

memes.label: Melantho
memes.description: Daughter of Dolius, sister of Melanthius, and mistress of Eurymachus — a disloyal servant of Penelope's household who insults Odysseus in his beggar disguise.

memes.label: Menelaus
memes.description: King of Sparta, husband of Helen, brother of Agamemnon — a veteran of the Trojan War who provides Telemachus with crucial intelligence about Odysseus's fate and the richest display of heroic hospitality in the Odyssey.

memes.label: Menis (Divine Wrath)
memes.description: The ancient Greek concept of divine wrath — the cosmic anger of the gods that drives epic narrative, from Achilles's menis in the Iliad to Poseidon's wrath against Odysseus and Zeus's judgment on the suitors.

memes.label: Mentor
memes.description: An aged Ithacan nobleman, loyal friend of Odysseus, whose form Athena repeatedly assumes to guide Telemachus and assist Odysseus — the archetype of the wise counsellor from whom the English word "mentor" derives.

memes.label: Metis (Cunning)
memes.description: The ancient Greek concept of cunning intelligence — the practical wisdom of strategy, disguise, and craft that defines Odysseus as a hero of mind rather than might.

memes.label: Minos
memes.description: Mythological king of Crete and judge of the dead in the Underworld, seen by Odysseus wielding a golden scepter among the shades.

memes.label: Moira (Fate)
memes.description: The ancient Greek concept of fate as an apportioned destiny — the portion of life and suffering allotted to each mortal by divine ordinance, often beyond even the gods' power to alter, and the moral architecture within which the Odyssey's characters choose and are judged.

memes.label: Mortal Characters
memes.description: An index of mortal human characters in the Odyssey wiki — heroes, kings, servants, suitors, and all who live and die within the epic's world.

memes.label: Most Referenced Pages
memes.description: A statistical ranking of the most-referenced pages across the Odyssey wiki, ordered by total cross-reference count.

memes.label: Mycenae
memes.description: The kingdom of Agamemnon, the commander of the Greek forces at Troy — a place never visited in the Odyssey but ever-present as a dark counter-example, the house where a returning king was murdered by his wife and her lover, and where his son Orestes exacted vengeance.

memes.label: Nausicaa
memes.description: The Phaeacian princess who discovers the shipwrecked Odysseus on the shores of Scheria and, with grace and courage, provides the hospitality that leads to his safe passage home.

memes.label: Nestor
memes.description: Aged king of Pylos, veteran of the Trojan War, renowned throughout the *Odyssey* for his wisdom, eloquence, and hospitality. Telemachus visits him in Book 3 seeking news of Odysseus.

memes.label: Nostos (Homecoming)
memes.description: The ancient Greek concept of homecoming — the hero's journey of return after war or wandering, a central narrative pattern of the Odyssey that gives the epic its name and structure.

memes.label: Oceanus
memes.description: The great river encircling the world in Greek cosmology, marking the boundary between the mortal realm and the outer darkness, referenced throughout the Odyssey.

memes.label: Odysseus
memes.description: King of Ithaca, hero of the Trojan War, and protagonist of the Odyssey — famed for his cunning intelligence (metis) and his ten-year journey home after the fall of Troy.

memes.label: Odysseus's Crew
memes.description: The company of Ithacan sailors who accompany Odysseus on his return from Troy — twelve ships and their crews whose progressive loss through disobedience, recklessness, and divine punishment forms the tragic arc of the wanderings.

memes.label: Ogygia
memes.description: The remote, paradisiacal island of the nymph Calypso where Odysseus was detained for seven years — a place of eternal sensual pleasure that threatened to erase his identity and his nostos.

memes.label: Olympus
memes.description: The mountain home of the Olympian gods, where Zeus convenes the divine councils that frame the Odyssey's action — a realm beyond mortal reach, yet intimately involved in every human event of the epic.

memes.label: Orestes
memes.description: Son of Agamemnon and Clytemnestra who avenged his father's murder by killing Aegisthus. Held up repeatedly in the *Odyssey* as the model of filial piety — the son who took action — in pointed contrast to Telemachus' initial passivity.

memes.label: Organizations Index
memes.description: A complete index of all groups, tribes, and collectives in Homer's Odyssey, dynamically generated from the wiki's organization entries.

memes.label: Orion
memes.description: A legendary giant hunter in Greek mythology, seen by Odysseus among the shades in the Underworld.

memes.label: Paris
memes.description: The Trojan prince whose abduction of Helen from Sparta triggered the Trojan War — a shadowy figure in the Odyssey, invoked as the paradigmatic violator of xenia whose transgression brought divine wrath upon an entire civilization and scattered every Achaean hero from his home.

memes.label: Parnassus
memes.description: The mountain where Odysseus received the scar on his thigh while hunting a wild boar in his youth, visited on a journey to his maternal grandfather Autolycus.

memes.label: Patroclus
memes.description: The beloved companion of Achilles, whose death at Troy is recalled in the *Odyssey* as a defining loss that echoes through the underworld and shapes how the dead are remembered by the living.

memes.label: Peiraeus
memes.description: A faithful Ithacan retainer entrusted by Telemachus with the guardianship of Theoclymenus and the safe-keeping of gifts from Sparta, whose loyalty anchors the domestic dimension of the prince's return.

memes.label: Penelope
memes.description: Queen of Ithaca, wife of Odysseus, and the archetype of marital fidelity in Western literature — she matches her husband in cunning, outwitting the suitors for three years through the shroud-weaving ruse.

memes.label: Perimedes
memes.description: A crew member of Odysseus named among those who assist with the underworld rites at the Cimmerian shore in Book XI.

memes.label: Phemius
memes.description: The bard of Odysseus's household in Ithaca, compelled to sing for the suitors but spared in the slaughter through Telemachus's intervention and his sacred status as a poet.

memes.label: Philoetius
memes.description: Loyal cowherd of Odysseus who aids in the slaughter of the suitors. Alongside Eumaeus, he is one of the two faithful servants crucial to Odysseus's revenge.

memes.label: Pisander
memes.description: One of the suitors of Penelope, son of Polyctor, who brings a precious necklace as a gift and is killed during the final battle in the hall.

memes.label: Pisistratus
memes.description: Son of Nestor, prince of Pylos, who accompanies Telemachus on his journey to Sparta in search of news of Odysseus.

memes.label: Pistis (Faithfulness)
memes.description: The ancient Greek concept of faithfulness and trust — the bonds of loyalty between husband and wife, master and servant, mortal and god that sustain the moral world of the Odyssey.

memes.label: Places Index
memes.description: A complete index of all locations and places in Homer's Odyssey, dynamically generated from the wiki's place entries.

memes.label: Poine (Revenge)
memes.description: The ancient Greek concept of retributive justice — the blood-price or vengeance that restores balance after wrongdoing, embodied in the Odyssey by Odysseus's slaughter of the suitors and Orestes's avenging of Agamemnon.

memes.label: Polyphemus
memes.description: The man-eating Cyclops and son of Poseidon whose blinding by Odysseus is both the hero's defining act of metis and the origin of Poseidon's wrath that drives the entire Odyssey.

memes.label: Poseidon
memes.description: God of the sea, earthquakes, and horses — the divine antagonist of the Odyssey whose wrath against Odysseus for blinding his son Polyphemus drives the hero's ten years of wandering.

memes.label: Proteus
memes.description: The shape-shifting sea-god of Egypt, called the Old Man of the Sea, from whom Menelaus wrests the prophecy that guides his return and reveals Odysseus's fate.
aliases: The Old Man of the Sea

memes.label: Pylos
memes.description: The coastal kingdom of Nestor in the southwestern Peloponnese, visited by Telemachus in Book 3 as the first stop on his journey to seek news of his father — a model of proper xenia and pious kingship.

memes.label: Reunion with Laertes
memes.description: The climactic recognition scene of the Odyssey (Book 24), in which Odysseus reveals himself to his aged father Laertes in the orchard through the scar and the memory of childhood trees, restoring the paternal bond before the final battle.

memes.label: Reunion with Penelope
memes.description: The long-delayed recognition scene between Odysseus and Penelope (Book 23) — the emotional climax of the Odyssey, where Penelope's final test of the marriage bed proves both her fidelity and her intelligence.

memes.label: Rhadamanthus
memes.description: A son of Zeus appointed as judge of the dead and ruler of Elysium in Homeric tradition, whose single mention in the Odyssey anchors the poem's vision of a blessed afterlife reserved for heroes.

memes.label: Same
memes.description: One of the Ionian islands under Odysseus's rule, contributing twenty-four suitors to Penelope's court; mentioned alongside Dulichium and Zacynthus as part of the realm.

memes.label: Scheria
memes.description: The prosperous island kingdom of the Phaeacians — the transitional realm between the world of adventure and Ithaca, whose people receive Odysseus with exemplary hospitality and provide his passage home.

memes.label: Scylla
memes.description: The six-headed sea monster who dwells in a cave opposite the whirlpool Charybdis — Odysseus must sail past her, sacrificing six men, in one of the epic's most famous scenes of impossible choice.

memes.label: Scylla and Charybdis
memes.description: "Odysseus's passage through the strait between the six-headed monster Scylla and the whirlpool Charybdis (Book 12), where he faces an impossible choice — lose six men to Scylla or lose the entire ship to the whirlpool — a trial where even metis cannot avert loss, only minimize it."

memes.label: Sisyphus
memes.description: A mythological king punished in Tartarus to eternally roll a boulder uphill, seen by Odysseus in the Underworld.

memes.label: Slaughter of the Suitors
memes.description: The climactic battle of the Odyssey (Book 22) in which Odysseus, aided by Telemachus, Eumaeus, and Athena, slaughters all 108 suitors for their violations of xenia and reclaims his household.

memes.label: Sparta
memes.description: The kingdom of Menelaus and Helen in the southern Peloponnese, visited by Telemachus in Book 4 — a realm of astonishing wealth and uneasy restoration, where the recovered king and his returned queen tell the stories that reveal Odysseus's fate.

memes.label: Tantalus
memes.description: A mythological king punished in Tartarus with eternal hunger and thirst, seen by Odysseus in the Underworld.

memes.label: Tiresias
memes.description: The blind Theban prophet whose shade Odysseus consults in the Underworld to learn his fate and the path home; unique among the dead in retaining full prophetic powers.

memes.label: Telemachus
memes.description: Son of Odysseus and Penelope, whose coming-of-age journey from passive youth to active hero structures the first four books of the Odyssey (the Telemachy), paralleling his father's nostos.

memes.label: Telemachus's Journey
memes.description: Telemachus's secret voyage from Ithaca to Pylos and Sparta (Books 2–4), guided by Athena in the guise of Mentor — the first journey the youth has ever undertaken, and the education in heroic identity, hospitality, and his father's legacy that transforms him from a passive boy into an active participant in the Odyssey's plot.

memes.label: Telepylus
memes.description: The fortified city of the cannibalistic Laestrygonians, where Odysseus loses eleven of his twelve ships in a catastrophic ambush.

memes.label: The Aegean Sea
memes.description: The body of water that both separates and connects the worlds of the Odyssey — the realm of Poseidon's wrath, the path of Odysseus's wanderings, and the ever-present barrier between the hero and his home.

memes.label: The Bow Contest
memes.description: Penelope's challenge to the suitors (Book 21) to string Odysseus's great bow and shoot through twelve axe-heads — the event that proves Odysseus's identity and triggers the slaughter.

memes.label: The Cattle of the Sun
memes.description: The final and most catastrophic episode of Odysseus's wanderings (Book 12), in which his starving crew, against his explicit command and their sworn oath, slaughters the sacred cattle of Helios — bringing divine vengeance that destroys the ship and all aboard except Odysseus himself.

memes.label: The Cave of the Nymphs
memes.description: The sacred cave on the shore of Ithaca where the Phaeacians deposit the sleeping Odysseus and his treasures (Book 13), and where Athena first reveals herself to the returned hero — the liminal space between the world of wandering and the world of home.

memes.label: The Cicones
memes.description: A Thracian people and allies of Troy, raided by Odysseus and his crew immediately after departing from Troy — the first episode of the hero's wanderings, whose disastrous aftermath sets the tone for the ten years of suffering to follow.

memes.label: The Cimmerians
memes.description: A mythical people dwelling in perpetual darkness at the edge of the Ocean stream, in whose land Odysseus performs the rites to summon the dead and gain entrance to the underworld in Book XI.

memes.label: The Council of the Gods
memes.description: The two divine councils (Books 1 and 5) that frame the Odyssey's action — Zeus's assembly at which Athena secures Hermes's mission to release Odysseus from Calypso, establishing the poem's theological framework of justice, free will, and divine intervention.

memes.label: The Cyclops Episode
memes.description: Odysseus's encounter with the man-eating Cyclops Polyphemus (Book 9) — his defining act of metis in escaping the cave, followed by the hubristic boast that earns Poseidon's undying wrath and drives the entire epic's ten years of wandering.

memes.label: The Grotto of the Nymphs
memes.description: A sacred seaside cave on Ithaca adjacent to the Harbor of Phorcys, dedicated to the Nereids, featuring perpetual waters, stone looms, and two entrances — one for gods, one for mortals.

memes.label: The Harbor of Phorcys
memes.description: A secluded natural harbor on the coast of Ithaca, sacred to the sea god Phorcys, where Odysseus first lands upon his return from Troy.

memes.label: The Island of the Laestrygonians
memes.description: The remote island where the cannibalistic Laestrygonian giants dwell, site of the destruction of eleven of Odysseus's ships — the greatest single loss of his fleet.

memes.label: The Laestrygonians
memes.description: The catastrophic encounter with the giant cannibals of Laestrygonia (Book 10), where Odysseus loses eleven of his twelve ships and their crews — the single greatest loss of life in the entire Odyssey, and the event that reduces the hero's fleet to a solitary vessel.

memes.label: The Lotus-Eaters
memes.description: A peaceful, hospitable people encountered by Odysseus on the Libyan coast whose consumption of the lotus fruit induces forgetfulness of home — the first non-hostile but still dangerous obstacle of the wanderings.

memes.label: The Mountain of Neriton
memes.description: The forested mountain of Ithaca whose summit divides the clouds, a defining feature of the island's topography referenced in Books IX, XIII, and XVII.

memes.label: Odyssey
memes.description: Homer's epic of homecoming — the poem of Odysseus's ten-year return from Troy to Ithaca, the foundational narrative of nostos in Western literature, and the source text of this wiki.

memes.label: The Palace of Odysseus
memes.description: The great hall of Odysseus on Ithaca — the central place of the Odyssey's domestic drama, where the suitors feast for four years, Penelope weaves and unweaves her shroud, the bow contest unfolds, and the slaughter restores order to the violated household.

memes.label: The Phaeacian Reception
memes.description: Odysseus's reception among the Phaeacians of Scheria (Books 6–8) — a three-book sequence encompassing Nausicaa's discovery of the shipwrecked hero, the hospitality of King Alcinous, the athletic games, Demodocus's songs, and the hero's revelation of his identity through the narration of his wanderings.

memes.label: The Phaeacians
memes.description: The seafaring people of Scheria, ruled by King Alcinous and Queen Arete, who receive Odysseus with perfect xenia after his shipwreck and provide the ship that finally carries him home to Ithaca.

memes.label: The Planctae
memes.description: The "Wandering Rocks" or "Clashing Rocks" — a deadly maritime hazard through which only the Argo has ever passed, avoided by Odysseus on Circe's advice.

memes.label: The Sirens
memes.description: Odysseus's passage by the island of the Sirens (Book 12), whose irresistible song lures sailors to their death — the first of the three sequential perils in Book 12, and the one the hero overcomes through foresight, self-binding, and the obedience of his crew.

memes.label: The Spring of Arethusa
memes.description: A freshwater spring on Ithaca whose dark waters are mentioned in Book XIII as a landmark near the Harbor of Phorcys and a pastoral meeting place.

memes.label: The Suitors
memes.description: The 108 noblemen from Ithaca and the surrounding islands who occupy Odysseus's palace during his absence, consuming his wealth and pursuing Penelope's hand — the primary human antagonists of the Odyssey.

memes.label: The Underworld (Nekyia)
memes.description: Odysseus's journey to the entrance of the Underworld (Book 11) to consult the shade of the seer Tiresias — the structural and theological center of the Odyssey, where the hero learns his fate and encounters the great dead of the Trojan War.

memes.label: The Wanderings of Odysseus
memes.description: The extended narrative of Odysseus's ten-year journey home from Troy, recounted in Books IX–XII of the Odyssey, encompassing encounters with the Cicones, Lotus-Eaters, Cyclops, Aeolus, Laestrygonians, Circe, the Underworld, Sirens, Scylla and Charybdis, and the Cattle of the Sun.

memes.label: Theoclymenus
memes.description: A fugitive seer from Argos, descended from the prophet Melampus, whom Telemachus takes aboard his ship at Pylos and brings to Ithaca, where his visions foretell the suitors' doom.

memes.label: Theoxeny (Divine Visitation)
memes.description: The ancient Greek belief that gods walk among mortals in disguise, testing human hospitality — a concept that gives every stranger-encounter in the Odyssey its underlying religious tension.

memes.label: Thesprotia
memes.description: A coastal region of Epirus in northwestern Greece, used by Odysseus in his false tales as a destination where he was richly rewarded — a real geographical referent that lends credibility to his fabricated identity.

memes.label: Thrinacia
memes.description: The sacred island where Helios pastures his immortal cattle — the site of the crew's fatal transgression in Book 12, and the last stop of Odysseus's wanderings before his solitary drift to Calypso's island.

memes.label: Time (Honor)
memes.description: The ancient Greek concept of honor as public recognition of worth — the social esteem that the Odyssey's characters strive to maintain, lose, and restore, and the invisible currency driving the poem's central conflicts.

memes.label: Tiresias
memes.description: The blind prophet of Thebes whose shade Odysseus consults in the Underworld — the only mortal to retain his mind after death, he reveals Odysseus's fate, the dangers ahead, and the final propitiation required for peace with Poseidon.

memes.label: Tityus
memes.description: A giant punished in Tartarus, seen by Odysseus in the Underworld. His body covers nine acres as vultures devour his liver.

memes.label: Troy
memes.description: The city on the plain of the Scamander whose ten-year siege by the Greeks defines the generation before the Odyssey — the war from which Odysseus has spent ten years returning, and the event whose survivors shape every kingdom Telemachus visits.

memes.label: Xenia (Guest-Friendship)
memes.description: The ancient Greek sacred custom of hospitality and guest-friendship, a central moral framework of Homer's Odyssey enforced by Zeus Xenios himself.

memes.label: Zacynthus
memes.description: A wooded island in the Ionian Sea, part of Odysseus's realm, contributing twenty suitors to the court of Penelope.

memes.label: Zeus
memes.description: King of the Olympian gods, god of the sky, thunder, and justice — arbiter of mortal fate and ultimate authority over the divine and human realms in the Odyssey.
